DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, FURTHER RESPONSES IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE CHRISTINE CRONIN AND DELPHINA CLINCH FOR DEPOSITION WITHIN 5 COURT DAYS OF THIS DATE. AS TO THE DOCUMENT REQUESTS, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO NOS. 1, 2, AND 3 AS THE DOCUMENTS NOT WITHIN THE CUSTODY AND CONTROL OF THE WITNESSES. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO NO. 2 AS IT SEEKS DOCUMENTS WHICH WOULD INVADE THE PRIVACY OF THIRD PERSONS WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO NO. 3 AS IT IS OVERLY BROAD AND INSUFFICIENTLY SPECIFIC. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS NOS. 1, 2, AND 5. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED, AND THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE. WHILE THE RESPONSE MAY BE DENIED BASED UPON LACK OF INFORMATION AND BELIEF, THE OBJECTION THAT THE REQUESTS CALL FOR EXPERT OPINION IS OVERRULED. FACTS MUST BE IDENTIFIED, IF THERE ARE ANY, BECAUSE FACTS ARE NOT CREATED BY EXPERTS. FACTS ARE INTERPRETED BY THE EXPERTS, AND THOSE FACTS MUST BE INCLUDED IN THE RESPONSE: "IF A RESPONDING PARTY GIVES LACK OF INFORMATION OR KNOWLEDGE AS A REASON FOR A FAILURE TO ADMIT ALL OR PART OF A REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, THAT PARTY SHALL STATE IN THE ANSWER THAT A REASONABLE INQUIRY CONCERNING THE MATTER IN THE REQUEST HAS BEEN MADE, AND THAT THE INFORMATION KNOWN OR READILY OBTAINABLE IS INSUFFICIENT TO ENABLE THAT PARTY TO ADMIT THE MATTER." [C.C.P. SECTION 2033(F)(1)(C) FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 5 COURT DAYS OF THE DATE OF THIS HEARING. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
